q: The pitch of a note is determined by its loudness.
A greater frequency of a sound wave results in a higher pitch.
The loudness of a note depends upon the amplitude of the wave that produces it.
If the amplitude of a sound wave is doubled, the loudness is two times greater.
Quality or timbre distinguishes two sounds of the same loudness and same pitch emitted by two different instruments.
The timbre of a sound depends on its waveform, which varies with the number of overtones.
Intensity is defined as the energy transmitted per second per unit area held normally to the direction of sound.
The intensity of a wave is inversely proportional to the square of the amplitude of the wave.
Loudness is an objective quantity that does not depend on the observer.
For a particular volume of sound, some may find it loud, while others might find it soft.
The amplitude of air molecules for a sound at the threshold of hearing is a very large value.
The Doppler equation is represented as $f_o = f_s \left( \frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s} \right)$.
Doppler ultrasound can provide information about the heartbeat of a fetus.
In the red shift phenomenon, the wavelength of light shifts towards the blue end of the spectrum when moving away.
Radar is used for applications like police radar and weather radar.
The energy transmitted per second per unit area is the definition of intensity.
The angular frequency of a sound wave is denoted by the symbol $\omega$.
Loudness depends on the subjective perception of the observer.
Sounds with the same frequency and pitch can still sound different due to their timbre.
The intensity of a sound wave is directly proportional to the square of the angular frequency $\omega$.
The Doppler effect cannot be used to examine organ functions like the kidney.
When a source recedes from an observer, the frequency heard is less than the source frequency.
The term $v_s$ represents the speed of the source.
The term $v_o$ represents the speed of the observer.
The waveform of a sound produced by a violin differs from that of a tuning fork.
High frequency sound is associated with lower pitch.
The Doppler effect is limited strictly to sound and electromagnetic radiation.
Frequency and pitch are unrelated concepts in acoustics.
The density of the medium is represented by $\rho$ in the intensity formula.
The Doppler equation $f_o = f_s \left( \frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s} \right)$ uses $v$ to represent the speed of sound.
